publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-09-19 18:09:35 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, Legal Documents, Newspapers, Photographs, Printed Materials.
did.abstract: Correspondence of Lewis Selye of Rochester, on Penn Yan business matters.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence of Lewis Selye of Rochester, on Penn Yan business matters, especially purchase of a fire engine, 1838-43; and of Stafford C. Cleveland, 1864-70, on local, state, and national politics. Other items include letters to D.C. Ayres from William H. Samson of Rochester about the will and signature of Jemima Wilkinson (the Publick Universal Friend); letters from Edwin C. Dinturff on local connections of Marcus Whitman and Joseph Smith; 1808 diary of travels of James Parker (1744-1829), early settler of Penn Yan; survey maps of townships in Yates County by Augustus Porter, Peter Loop, Jabez French, and others, 1789-1815; later lot surveys; assessment record, town of Benton, 1817; letters of Bert S. Elliot on Penn Yan in the 1890's and on his career as a printer with U.S. Government Printing Office and other agencies. There are also miscellaneous genealogical data, early deeds, and legal documents, photographs, broadsides, engravings, historical notes and clippings, and letters.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Sidney Ayres papers, #1605. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

unittitle.extref: Link to a copy of the finding aid. (PDF file)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper card: "The New Illustrated Weekly Family Newspaper of Leon and Harriet Lewis." Penn Yan, New York (Yates County).

did.unittitle: EXTRA YATES CUNTY CHRONICLE - Small Broadside

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shooting of President McKinley, Sept. 7, 1901. Original and Xerox made here. The latter to be transferred to general Broadside Collection, for use of researchers. Original, in fragile condition, to be retained with papers.

did.unittitle: Edwin C. Dinturff to Sidney E. Ayres

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Sidney E. Ayres from Edwin C. Dinturff of Syracuse, concerning the location of Marcus Whitman's birthplace. 1 p. MS.

did.unittitle: Edwin C. Dinturff to Sidney E. Ayres

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Sidney E. Ayres from Edwin C. Dinturff, alleging influence of Rachel and Margaret Malin on Joseph Smith. 4 pp. MSS., April 30, 1931.

did.unittitle: Azariah S. Root to F. C. Davis

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Enclosed in April 30th letter is a letter to Mr. F. C. Davis of Syracuse from Azariah S. Root, Oberlin College Librarian, concerning the Solomon Spaulding MSS. Alleged to be similar to the Book of Mormon. 1 p. typescript, May 5, 1925

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also enclosed, an unidentified newspaper clipping (Feb. 24, no year), "Hill Where Book of Mormon Originated Bought by Church."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Report or paper by Dinturff, "Who Was the Autor [sic] of the Book of Mormon?" 20 pp. MSS. Undated. Illustrated with dull finish photograph of portrait of Jemima Wilkinson, presumably the one in the possession of the Yates County Historical Society in Penn Yan, and three newspaper clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three letters to Ayres from Bert S. Elliott of Rockville Centre, who had been a resident of Penn Yan in the 1890's and an employee of the Yates County Chronicle, concerning persons and places in the village of his day and his life work (as a compositor and proofreader) for the Government Printing Office in Washington and elsewhere in that city and in Colorado Springs. November 20, 1951, 10pp. typescript; October 23, 1953, 2 pp. typescript; April 29, 1955, 1 pc. MS (in pencil).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One pictorial "Romance Map" of Finger Lakes Region; compiled by Sidney E. Ayres and Mrs. Walter A. Hendricks, drawn by C. M. Plaisted and copyrighted by Sidney E. Ayres, 1934; Penn Yan Printing Company, Inc., Penn Yan, N.Y. 1957 (revision) Printed in color. 19" x 24 3/4" Map coll. # 104

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No author, no date. Several handwritten corrections. 22 pp. typescript carbon.

did.unittitle: Program for celebration at Penn Yan of the 40th Anniversary of the discovery of America.
"Bert S. Elliott Recalls 1892 Columbus Day Celebration"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clipping, from column, The People's Forum

did.unittitle: The Student's Hand-Book of Yates County

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hard cover volume, compiled and edited by William V. Stork. Penn Yan. 154 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One pictorial "Romance Map" of Finger Lakes Region; compiled by Sidney E. Ayres and Mrs. Walter A. Hendricks, drawn by C. M. Plaisted and copyrighted by Sidney E. Ayres, 1934; Penn Yan Printing Company, Inc., Penn Yan, N.Y. 1957 (revision) Printed in color. 19" x 24 3/4" Map coll. # 104
